title: head-to-head rollup: lz-refactor (recommend / invoke_skill) vs mattpocock code-review
model: claude-opus-4-8 @ high, --setting-sources project, k=3 (D-10), recommend mode, synthetic whole-file-diff base

arm_aliases.lz-refactor: run-e2e.mjs arm invoke_skill (/lz-tdd:lz-refactor, recommend mode, read-only)

caveats: D-02: code-review is designed for INCREMENTAL diffs; it was fed the whole target file as a diff vs an empty-root baseline -- slightly off its natural grain (the price of an equal-input comparison).
D-03: nx cells have no originating issue/PRD, so code-review's Spec sub-agent reported 'no spec available' and skipped; on the kata cell it engaged, treating the canonical kata as an implicit spec.
A1: token/cost headline = total_cost_usd + per-model model_usage (rolls up code_review's sub-agents); main-context input/output_tokens under-count code_review.
A4: the code_review arm loads the third-party mattpocock plugin (~22 skill descriptions) into context -- minor inherent context inflation.

rows.idiom_pattern: authentic, broad (Fowler + Kerievsky routing + declined GoF)

rows.distinct_value: caught a genuine correctness bug lz-refactor missed: unescaped-dot regex /.tsx?$/ (1 of 3 runs)

rows.idiom_pattern: authentic; reached the FP axis (Replace Loop with Pipeline on T3)
rows.miss: T4 groupImports reduce->Map missed in all 3 runs

rows.idiom_pattern: none; 12-smell baseline has no loop/pipeline concept
rows.miss: dismissed T3 for-loops as out-of-scope; missed T4

rows.over_under_engineering: proportionate runs 1&3 (Feathers-first, decline until growth); run-2 mildly over-eager (APPLY polymorphism early)
unguarded on the pattern (floated polymorphism with no APPLY/DECLINE net-cost -- partial)
rows.distinct_value: Spec axis verified behavioral faithfulness to the canonical kata (0 defects)

idiom_pattern_vocabulary_breadth_headlin.note: The ~139-vs-12 breadth asymmetry is EXPECTED and is the headline on the idiom/pattern axis -- reported, NOT normalized away (D-04).

code_review.detail: 12 Fowler code smells; no refactoring/pattern/idiom vocabulary

shared_blind_spot: Both arms missed T4 (groupImports reduce->Map group-by) in every cr-rlu run.
verdict: Empirical finding, not a pre-assumed win. The two skills produce DIFFERENTLY-SHAPED lift. lz-refactor is the book-grounded refactoring specialist: it routes each smell to a named, book-authentic Fowler/Kerievsky refactoring, sequences them smallest-safest-first, reaches the functional axis (Replace Loop with Pipeline), makes an explicit net-cost APPLY/DECLINE proportionality judgment on tempting patterns (correctly declining the over-engineering traps), and does so at LOWER cost with zero sub-agents. code_review names the same underlying smells authentically (Fowler ch.3) but gives generic prose fixes with no named-refactoring/pattern/idiom vocabulary, no sequencing, and (on the kata) an unguarded pattern suggestion -- yet it delivers distinct OFF-AXIS value lz-refactor does not attempt: it caught a genuine correctness bug (unescaped-dot regex) and its Spec axis verified behavioral faithfulness. Where lz-refactor's book-grounded catalog MEASURABLY differs: named-refactoring lift (11-12 vs 0), functional-axis coverage (structurally unreachable for a 12-smell baseline), pattern proportionality (net-cost decline vs unguarded floating), and cost (~30-45% cheaper). The ~139-vs-12 vocabulary breadth is the headline on the idiom/pattern axis -- real and expected, a defect of neither.
